name: VisPy (High-Performance Scientific Visualization)

performance.notes: OpenGL/GLSL shaders; GPU-accelerated; real-time rendering of millions of points

agent_readiness.notes: GPU-accelerated Python scientific visualization via OpenGL. from vispy import app, scene. canvas = scene.SceneCanvas(keys='interactive', show=True). view = canvas.central_widget.add_view(). scatter = scene.visuals.Markers(). scatter.set_data(positions, face_color='white', size=2). view.add(scatter). app.run(). Backends: Qt, pyqt6, wx, glfw, sdl2. 2D/3D: scatter, lines, images, volumes, meshes. Real-time updates via canvas.update(). OpenGL shaders for custom visuals. Used by napari as rendering backend. Steep learning curve but unmatched performance.

gotchas: Very steep learning curve — requires OpenGL/shader knowledge for custom visuals
Requires display (headless is possible but complex)
Documentation is sparse and examples can be outdated
Qt/pyqt dependency required for interactive mode
best_when: Real-time visualization of millions of points (EEG, particle systems, LiDAR streams)
Custom GPU-accelerated visual types via GLSL shaders
Embedded scientific visualization in Qt applications
When napari plugin development requires vispy visuals
avoid_when: Interactive data exploration (plotly or bokeh simpler)
summary: VisPy is a GPU-accelerated Python visualization library via OpenGL. scene.SceneCanvas() creates a window. scene.visuals.Markers().set_data(positions) renders scatter plots. Real-time update via canvas.update(). Handles millions of points in real time. Used by napari as rendering backend. Steep learning curve requiring OpenGL/shader knowledge. Best for real-time streaming visualization of large datasets (EEG, LiDAR, particle systems). Use plotly/bokeh for typical interactive data viz.
